title: Blue optoelectronic device
abstract: The present invention relates to a blue optoelectronic device comprising a blue dye-sensitized metal oxide electrode and an iodine containing electrolyte comprising at least one kind of special ionic liquid which is not an iodide, the preparation of said device including the preparation of the blue dye-sensitized metal oxide electrode and a preferred sensitizing solution.
claims:
Claims Patent Claims 1. A blue optoelectronic device comprising a blue electrode comprising a metal oxide sensitized with a compound of formula (I), wherein R and R # are independently of each other a straight- chain or branched alkyl group having 1 to 12 C atoms, R and R 2 are independently of each other H, a straight-chain or branched alkyl group having 1 to 12 C atoms or R 1 and R 2 jointly form a cyclic structure, R 3 , R 4 and R 5 are independently of each other H, a straight-chain or branched alkyl group having 1 to 12 C atoms, a straight-chain or branched alkenyl group having 2 to 12 C atoms or an aryl group having 6 to 12 C atoms which may be substituted, n is 0 or 1 , m is 0, 1 , 2, 3 or 4, p is 0, 1 or 2, m+p is 1 , 2, 3, 4, 5 or 6, and/or salts, tautomers, stereoisomers and/or solvates thereof and a counter electrode and an electrolyte comprising at least one compound of formula (II) or (III) M a+ [B(X)x(CN)4-x-y(F) y ] a - (II) M a+ [P(Rf)z(F) 6-z ]a " (III) 

 in which M a is an organic cation, X denotes each independently R 6 , R f or H, R 6 denotes straight-chain or branched alkyl groups having 1 to 4 C atoms or straight-chain or branched alkoxy groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, Rf denotes straight-chain or branched perfluoroalkyl groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, C 6 F 5 , C 6 H 5 , partially fluorinated phenyl or phenyl which is monosubstituted or disubstituted by perfluoroalkyl groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, a is 1 , 2, 3 or 4, x is 0, 1 or 2, y is 0, 1 or 2, x+y < 4 and z is 1 , 2 or 3, and iodine wherein the initial concentration c of molecular iodine given in weight % related to the distance d given in Î¼Î¹Î· between the blue sensitized metal oxide electrode and the counter electrode is 0.005 < c/d < 0.2. A device according to claim 1 which is a solar cell, an optical detector, a photoreceptor, a photodiode, a photomultiplier, a photo resistor, a photo detector, a light sensitive detector, a solid- state triode, a transistor, an integrated circuit, a field-quench device, a light-emitting device, a laser, a laser diode, a plasmon emitting device, an electrophotography device or a wave converter. A device according to claim 1 or 2 which is a solar cell. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 3 wherein the sensitized metal oxide comprise a co-adsorbent. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 4 wherein the lambda max of the sensitized metal oxide electrode is > 540 nm. 6. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 5 wherein the co-adsorbent is deoxycholic acid and/or a-cyclodextrin. 7. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 6 wherein an 5 organic iodide is comprised in the electrolyte. 8. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 7 wherein the organic cation M a+ within the compound of formula (II) or (III) in the electrolyte is an organic cation selected from the group - j O comprising sulfonium, oxonium, ammonium, phosphonium, uronium, thiouronium, guanidinium cations or heterocyclic cations. 9. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 8 wherein the organic cation M a+ within the compound of formula (II) or (III) in the electrolyte is a heterocyclic cation which corresponds to the formula (8) [HetN] z+ (8) where HetN z+ denotes a heterocyclic cation selected from the 0 group imidazolium 1 H-pyrazolium 3H-pyrazolium 4H-pyrazolium 1-pyrazolinium 2-pyrazolinium 3-pyrazolinium 2,3-dihydroimidazolinium 0 4,5-dihydroimidazolinium 2,5-dihydroimidazolinium pyrrolidinium 1 ,2,4-triazolium 1 ,2,4-triazolium 1 ,2,3-triazolium quinoxalinium benzimidazolium where the substituents R 1 ' to R 4 ' each, independently of one another, denote H, straight-chain or branched alkyl having 1-20 C atoms, straight-chain or branched alkenyl having 2-20 C atoms and one or more double bonds, straight-chain or branched alkynyl having 2-20 C atoms and one or more triple bonds, saturated, partially or fully unsaturated cycloalkyl having 3-7 C atoms, which may be substituted by straight-chain or alkyl groups having 1-6 C atoms, saturated, partially or fully unsaturated heteroaryl, heteroaryl-Cr C 6 -alkyl or aryl-Ci-C 6 -alkyl and R 2' denote additionally F, CI, Br, I, -CN, -OR", -N(R') 2 , -P(O)(R') 2 , - P(0)(OR') 2 , -P(0)(N(R') 2 ) 2 , -C(0)R', -C(0)OR', -C(0)X', - C(0)N(R') 2 , -S0 2 N(R') 2 , -S0 2 OH, -SO 2 X\ -SR', -S(0)R', -S0 2 R' and/or N0 2 , with the proviso that R 1 ', R 3 ', R 4 ' are in this case independently of each other H and/or a straight-chain or branched alkyl having 1-20 C atoms, straight-chain or branched alkenyl having 2-20 C atoms and one or more double bonds, where the substituents R , R 2 , R 3 and/or R together may also form a ring system, where one to three substituents R 1 to R 4 may be fully substituted by halogens, in particular -F and/or -CI, and one or more substituents R r to R 4 may be partially substituted by halogens, in 

 particular -F and/or -CI, and/or by -OH, -OR', N(R') 2 , -CN, -C(O)OH, -C(O)OR', -C(O)R', -C(O)N(R') 2 , -SO 2 N(R') 2 , -C(0)X', - SO 2 OH, -SO 2 X', -SR', -S(O)R', -SO 2 R', -NO 2 , but where R 1 ' and R 4 cannot simultaneously be fully substituted by halogens and where, in the substituents R 1 to R 4 , one or two non-adjacent carbon atoms which are not bonded to the heteroatom may be replaced by atoms and/or atom groups selected from the -O-, -S-, -S(O)-, -SO 2 -, -SO 2 O-, -C(O)-, -C(O)O-, -N + (R') 2 -, -P(O)R'O-, - C(O)NR'-, -SO 2 NR'-, -OP(O)RO-, -P(O)(N(R') 2 )NR'-, -P(R') 2 =N- or -P(O)R'-, where R' each independently is H, non-fluorinated, partially fluorinated or perfluorinated straight-chain or branched Ci- to ds- alkyl, saturated C3- to C 7 -cycloalkyl, non-substituted or substituted phenyl and X' each independently is halogen. 10. A device according to one or more of claims 1 to 9 wherein the anion of the compound of formula (II) or (III) is selected from the group tetracyanoborate, monohydridotricyanoborate, monofluorotricyanoborate, dihydridodicyanoborate, difluorodicyanoborate, methyltricyanoborate, ethyltncyanoborate, methoxytricyanoborate, ethoxytricyanoborate, trifluoromethyldicyanofluoroborate, pentafluoroethyldicyanofluoroborate, trifluoromethylcyanodifluoroborate, pentafluoroethylcyanodifluoroborate, tris(pentafluoroethyl)trifluorophosphate, bis(pentafluoroethyl)tetrafluorophosphate, pentafluoroethylpentafluorophosphate, tris(heptafluoropropyl)trifluorophosphate, bis(heptafluoropropyl)tetrafluorophosphate, heptafluoropropylpentafluorophosphate, 

 tris(nonafluorobutyl)trifluorophosphate, bis(nonafluorobutyl)tetrafluorophosphate, nonafluorobutylpentafluorophosphate. 11. A process for the manufacture of a blue dye-sensitized metal oxide electrode wherein a metal oxide electrode is brought into contact with a sensitizing solution comprising a compound of formula (I) according to claim 1 or a mixture of a co-adsorbent and a compound of formula (I) according to claim 1 in a solvent having a dispersive (6d) component of 16 Â± 2 MPA 05 , a polar (Î´Ï) component of 7 Â± 2 MPA 0 5 and a hydrogen bonding (5h) component of 17 Â± 3 MPA 0 5 . 12. A process for the manufacture of a blue dye-sensitized metal oxide electrode wherein a metal oxide electrode is brought into contact with a first solution comprising a co-adsorbent and further with a sensitizing solution comprising a compound of formula (I) according to claim 1 in a solvent having a dispersive (5d) component of 16 Â± 2 MPA 0 5 , a polar (Î´Ï) component of 7 Â± 2 MPA 0 5 and a hydrogen bonding (6h) component of 17 Â± 3 MPA 0 5 . 13. A process according to claim 11 or 12 wherein the solvent is selected from the group 1-propanol and 2-propanol. 14. A process according to one or more of claims 11 to 13 wherein the molar ratio of the co-adsorbent relative to the compound of formula (I) is > 5:1. 15. A process according to one or more of claims 11 to 14 wherein the molar ratio of the co-adsorbent relative to the compound of formula (I) is 10:1 to 30:1. 16. A process according to one or more of claims 11 to 15 wherein the co-adsorbent is deoxycholic acid and/or a-cyclodextrin. 17. A sensitizing solution comprising a compound of formula (I) according to claim 1 and a co-adsorbent in a solvent having a dispersive (5d) component of 16 Â± 2 MPAÂ° 5 , a polar (Î´Ï) component of 7 Â± 2 MPAÂ° 5 and a hydrogen bonding (Î´Î·) component of 17 Â± 3 MPAÂ° 5 wherein the molar ratio of the co- adsorbent relative to the compound of formula (I) is > 5:1. 18. A process for the manufacture of a device according to one or more of claims 1 to 10 comprising the steps of - providing a blue metal oxide electrode manufactured according to one or more of claims 11 to 16 as a first electrode; - providing a counter electrode as a second electrode; - assembling the first and the second electrode through sealing and building an internal space d between the electrodes and - filling the distance d with the electrolyte comprising at least one compound of formula (II) or (III) - M a+ [B(X)x(CN) 4-x-y (F) y ] a - (II) - M a+ [P(Rf) z (F) 6-z ] a - (III) in which M a+ is an organic cation, X denotes each independently R 6 , R f or H, R 6 denotes straight-chain or branched alkyl groups having 1 to 4 C atoms or straight-chain or branched alkoxy groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, Rf denotes straight-chain or branched perfluoroalkyi groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, C 6 F 5 , C6H 5) partially fluorinated phenyl or phenyl which is monosubstituted or disubstituted by perfluoroalkyi groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, a is 1 , 2, 3 or 4, x is 0, 1 or 2, y is 0, 1 or 2, x+y < 4 and 

 z is 1 , 2 or 3, and iodine wherein the initial concentration c of molecular iodine given in weight % related to the distance d given in Î¼Î¹Î· between the first electrode and the second electrode is 0.005 < c/d < 0.2. 9. A process for the manufacture of a device according to one or more of claims 1 to 10 comprising the steps of - providing a non-sensitized metal oxide electrode as a first electrode; - providing a counter electrode as a second electrode; - assembling the first and the second electrode through sealing and building an internal space d between the electrodes; - sensitizing the metal oxide with a sensitizing solution according to claim 17 or adsorbing a co-adsorbent followed by sensitizing with a sensitizing solution comprising a compound of formula (I) as described in claim 1 in a solvent having a dispersive (5d) component of 16 Â± 2 MPA0.5, a polar (Î´Ï) component of 7 Â± 2 MPAÂ° 5 and a hydrogen bonding (5h) component of 17 Â± 3 MPAÂ° 5 ; - cleaning the sensitized metal oxide with a cleaning solution and - filling the distance d with the electrolyte comprising at least one compound of formula (II) or (III) - M a+ [B(X) x (CN)4-x-y(F) y ] a - (II) - M a+ [P(R f ) z (F) 6-z ] a - (III) in which M a+ is an organic cation, X denotes each independently R 6 , R f or H, R 6 denotes straight-chain or branched alkyl groups having 1 to 4 C atoms or straight-chain or branched alkoxy groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, R f denotes straight-chain or branched perfluoroalkyl groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, C 6 F 5 , C 6 H 5 , partially fluorinated phenyl or 

 phenyl which is monosubstituted or disubstituted by perfluoroalkyi groups having 1 to 4 C atoms, a is 1 , 2, 3 or 4, x is 0, 1 or 2, y is 0, 1 or 2, x+y < 4 and z is 1 , 2 or 3, and iodine wherein the initial concentration c of molecular iodine given in weight % related to the distance d given in m between the sensitized first electrode and the second electrode is 0.005 < c/d < 0.2.
